evidence: 5 experts covering 7 of 8 sub-domains
Strong coverage of evidence verification, fact-checking, claim classification, and source credibility
Rabbany and Hanselowski cover web retrieval and multi-provider aggregation
Augenstein covers multi-domain verification and explainability
Nakov covers propaganda detection and media bias
gaps: MCP and agent runtime integration expertise only partially covered by Hanselowski
No dedicated specialist for information extraction from web pages
Multilingual concerns acknowledged but not deeply operationalized

evidence: All profiles have complete reasoning kernels, question playbooks, and advantage knowledge bases
Rabbany and Hanselowski have the deepest profiles with 5-6 canonical works and 5 playbook entries
Atanasova has detailed playbook entries and rich advantage KB
Augenstein and Nakov are thinner with only 3 canonical works and 2 playbook entries each
gaps: Augenstein and Nakov profiles need more canonical works and quote bank entries
No profile reaches authoritative standalone reference work level
Some blind spots sections are generic rather than specific to the domain

evidence: Complete CLI tools (search-web, browse-page, verify-claim, evidence-report) plus MCP server
Working provider abstraction with DdgsProvider and SearxngProvider
46 unit + integration tests passing with benchmark fixtures
Dockerfile, docker-compose, deployment scripts, and platform integration files
Evidence-aware heuristic v3 verification model with documented score weights
Error handling for provider failures, network errors, and unknown providers
gaps: No rate limiting, retry with backoff, or circuit breaker for provider calls
No async implementation for concurrent provider queries
No structured logging or monitoring hooks for production
English-only conflict markers without multilingual support
No type checking or linting enforced in CI

evidence: Core workflow is functional end-to-end: search-verify-report pipeline works with live providers
Evidence report surface produces structured output with verdict rationale, stance summaries, and next steps
Dual-provider aggregation with cross-validated deduplication works
Page-aware verification genuinely improves classification over snippet heuristics
Source quality heuristics provide reasonable first-order signal
gaps: Verification is purely heuristic - no NLP-based stance detection or entailment reasoning
Conflict detection relies on English-language markers only
Uncalibrated confidence thresholds (hand-tuned, not benchmarked against gold standard)
No claim decomposition for compound claims
Binary provider diversity without reliability weighting
Naive browse-page extractor without main content detection or boilerplate removal

recommendations: Add NLP-based stance detection or sentence similarity for verification beyond keyword overlap
Add claim decomposition for compound claims
Add confidence calibration against a gold standard dataset
Implement async provider calls for concurrent queries
Add rate limiting and retry logic for provider resilience
Deepen Augenstein and Nakov expert profiles with more canonical works and playbook entries
Improve browse-page with main content extraction and boilerplate removal
